audit_items.proof_trace: Within uprv_collation_root_cleanup(), SharedObject::clearPtr(rootSingleton) calls ptr->removeRef() (potentially deleting the object) and then sets ptr = NULL — this second step writes to the pointer variable (rootSingleton) only, not through the freed pointer, so no use-after-free occurs. The NULL guard inside clearPtr prevents double-free; initOnce.reset() on L42 operates on an entirely separate static global and never touches rootSingleton. All three phases confirm no UAF or double-free within the function body.
